title: Run limma analysis per contrast on filtered proteins — run_filtered_limma_analysis
text: function subsets DAList contrast according filtered_proteins_per_contrast fits limma model separately. useful proteins filtered differently per contrast. model fitting, function computes rolling standard deviations (moving SDs) log fold changes based intensity-sorted proteins, computes z-scores.

title: Run limma analysis per contrast on filtered proteins — run_filtered_limma_analysis
code: run_filtered_limma_analysis(   DAList,   design_formula = ~0 + group,   pval_thresh = 0.05,   lfc_thresh = 1,   adj_method = "BH",   contrasts_file = NULL,   binsize = "auto",   plot_movingSD = FALSE )

title: Run limma analysis per contrast on filtered proteins — run_filtered_limma_analysis
text: DAList DAList object containing full data per-contrast filtered proteins. design_formula formula model design (e.g., ~0 + group, ~0 + cell:treatment). pval_thresh P-value threshold used defining significance. Default = 0.05. lfc_thresh Log2 fold change threshold significance. Default = 1. adj_method Adjustment method multiple testing ("BH", "", "none", etc). Default = "BH". contrasts_file Optional CSV file contrast definitions present DAList. row contain full contrast statement form Label = expression. label (left-hand side) may string; need encode group names. binsize Either integer moving window size, "auto" (default) select appropriate bin size automatically. plot_movingSD Logical. TRUE (default), plot moving SD curves contrast.

title: Run limma analysis per contrast on filtered proteins — run_filtered_limma_analysis
text: input DAList, updated per-contrast model fits results, moving SDs, logFC z-scores.

title: Run limma analysis per contrast on filtered proteins — run_filtered_limma_analysis
text: contrast, sidecar list saved DAList$tags$per_contrast[[label]]$contrast_info downstream functions (e.g., write_limma_tables()) need parse labels. contrast_info contains: label: contrast label (left-hand side) contrast_expression_raw: RHS written file contrast_expression: RHS actually passed limma (translation) design_formula: design formula character string group_col: grouping column applicable (NULL pure interaction designs) factors: factor names participating simple two-factor interaction (e.g., c("cell","treatment")) design_columns_involved: model-matrix columns referenced translated expression involved_levels: levels group_col appearing expression (applicable)

title: Run limma analysis per contrast on filtered proteins — run_filtered_limma_analysis

Finally, users can use title_column argument change title protein intensity (plots right report). default, values uniprot_id column annotation used. avoid plotting issues, values user-provided column truncated 15 characters use title: values must remain unique truncation. title_column supplied, added table_columns displayed table well.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Using defaults   write_limma_plots(DAList,                     grouping_column = "treatment")    # Adjust size of report   write_limma_plots(DAList,                     grouping_column = "treatment",                     height = 1500,                     width = 1500)   # Customize output directory  write_limma_plots(DAList,                    grouping_column = "treatment",                    output_dir = "DA_results")   # Add titles to the intensity plot,  # using the values in the protein_id column  # in the annotation of the DAList,  # and display additional columns in the table  write_limma_plots(DAList,                    grouping_column = "treatment",                    table_columns = "description",                    title_column = "protein_id") } # }

title: Write a post-normalization evaluation report for a single method — write_norm_eval_report
text: DAList DAList containing already-normalized matrices (per-contrast global). norm_label Character scalar used list name plots (e.g., "cycloess"). grouping_column Name metadata column giving sample groups. Must contain >= 2 groups. output_dir Directory save PDF (created missing). Default: working directory. filename File name PDF (must end .pdf). Default: "norm_eval_report.pdf". overwrite Overwrite existing file? Default FALSE. suppress_zoom_legend Remove legend zoomed log2 ratio plot? Default FALSE. use_ggrastr Use ggrastr MD plots (installed) reduce file size? Default FALSE. input_is_log2 Logical, whether matrices already log2 scale metrics expect log2 (visualization/relative; flag unused kept symmetry). contrasts Optional character vector: restrict contrasts data_per_contrast present. sample_id_col Optional name metadata column whose values match matrix colnames. NULL, function try auto-detect suitable column. groups_override Optional named vector group labels names equal matrix column names. supplied, overrides grouping_column/sample_id_col matching. metrics_csv Optional path CSV file per-sample normalization metrics (e.g., PCV, PMAD, PEV, COR, mean intensity) written. NULL, metrics exported CSV.

code: write_norm_report(   DAList,   grouping_column = NULL,   output_dir = NULL,   filename = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   suppress_zoom_legend = FALSE,   use_ggrastr = FALSE,   input_is_log2 = FALSE,   contrasts = NULL,   sample_id_col = NULL,   groups_override = NULL,   metrics_csv = NULL,   per_contrast = FALSE,   include_MD_plots = TRUE )

title: Create a normalization report (optionally contrast-aware) — write_norm_report
text: DAList DAList. grouping_column Name metadata column gives sample groups (used within-group cyclic loess). Must contain least two groups. output_dir Directory save report (created missing). Defaults current working directory. filename File name report (must end .pdf). Default "normalization_report.pdf". overwrite Overwrite report already exists? Default FALSE. suppress_zoom_legend Remove legend zoomed log2-ratio plot? Default FALSE. use_ggrastr Use ggrastr MD plots reduce size (installed)? Default FALSE. input_is_log2 Logical. TRUE, indicates per-contrast (global) input matrices already log2 scale methods expect log2 input. Default FALSE. contrasts Optional character vector: per_contrast = TRUE data_per_contrast present, restrict report contrasts. Default = contrasts data_per_contrast. sample_id_col Optional name metadata column whose values match matrix column names. NULL, function attempts auto-detect suitable column (e.g., "sample"). groups_override Optional named vector group labels names equal matrix column names. supplied, overrides group labels derived grouping_column sample_id_col. metrics_csv Optional path CSV file per-sample normalization metrics (PCV, PMAD, PEV, COR, mean intensity, etc.) written. NULL, metrics exported CSV. per_contrast Logical. TRUE DAList$data_per_contrast present, produce separate set normalization plots per contrast. FALSE (default), ignore data_per_contrast evaluate normalization globally using DAList$data. include_MD_plots Logical. TRUE (default), include extended MD/MA diagnostic pages contrast (global). Set FALSE omit plots, can substantially reduce PDF file size.

title: Create a normalization report (optionally contrast-aware) — write_norm_report
text: Invisibly returns input DAList.

title: Create a normalization report (optionally contrast-aware) — write_norm_report
text: default, normalization evaluated globally DAList$data, typically raw (unnormalized) expression matrix. per_contrast = TRUE DAList$data_per_contrast exists, function instead evaluates normalization separately contrast, using matrices slot (leaving DAList$data untouched). report always includes per-sample normalization metrics (PCV, PMAD, PEV, COR, mean intensity, etc.). include_MD_plots = TRUE, additionally shows set MD/MA-style diagnostics normalization method: classic MD plots (points + loess trend) trend-MD plots (points, compare bias across methods) 2D-binned (heatmap-style) MD plots Delta-trend curves: difference method's loess fit raw log2 trend, highlighting -/-correction relative input residual plots: distributions loess residuals per method log2FC distribution plots (per method), visualize normalization compresses preserves fold-change variation

title: Create a quality control report — write_qc_report
text: DAList DAList. color_column name column metadata gives information color samples plots within report. supplied, samples color. label_column Optional. name column within targets data frame contains labels use plotting figures. supplied, defaults using column names data processed_data. ensure good plot formatting, sample labels truncated 20 characters, function give error sample labels unique. output_dir Directory save report (created missing). Defaults current working directory. filename file name report saved. Must end .pdf. default "QC_Report.pdf" filename provided. overwrite Overwrite report already exists? Default FALSE. top_proteins number variable proteins use PCA dendrogram clustering. Default 500. standardize input data standardized mean 0 std.dev 1 performing PCA dendrogram clustering? input data yet standardized, TRUE. Default TRUE. pca_axes numeric vector length 2 lists PC axes plot. Default c(1,2), plot first two principal components. dist_metric metric used define distance dendrogram clustering. Default "euclidean". See stats::dist options. clust_method agglomeration method use dendrogram clustering. Default "complete", See stats::hclust options. show_all_proteins proteins shown missing value heatmap, missing data? Default F (missing data).

title: Create a quality control report — write_qc_report
text: report created successfully, invisibly returns input DAList.

title: Create a quality control report — write_qc_report
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Color samples according to group identities # in the "treatment" column of the metadata write_qc_report(DAList,                 color_column = "treatment")  # Change the default directory and file names write_qc_report(DAList,                 color_column = "treatment",                 output_dir = "my/chosen/directory",                 filename = "my_report.pdf")  # Overwrite an existing report write_qc_report(DAList,                 color_column = "treatment",                 overwrite = T)  # Customize PCA and clustering plots write_qc_report(DAList,                 color_column = "treatment",                 top_proteins = 1000,                 pca_aces = c(2,3),                 dist_metric = "manhattan",                 clust_method = "average")  } # }
